description: Strengthen E2E test assertions and resource cleanup: tighten overly broad count assertions in audit-logging and analyze-site tests, add try-finally cleanup for raw WebSocket in dispatch-resilience test, and fix resource leak risk in sdk-version standalone test.

userStories.title: Tighten audit-logging entry count assertions to use exact or filtered counts
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want audit-logging.e2e.ts assertions to use exact entry counts so that spurious entries or missed operations are caught by the test.
acceptanceCriteria: Line ~88 in e2e/audit-logging.e2e.ts uses filtered entry count (filter by tool names 'e2e-test_echo' and 'e2e-test_failing_tool') and asserts exact count instead of toBeGreaterThanOrEqual
Lines ~111-112 use filtered counts by success/failure of the specific tools invoked, not a blanket >= 1
Line ~138 uses a filtered count or exact assertion for the specific tool tested
Tests still pass against the current server behavior
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/audit-logging.e2e.ts

The test invokes exactly two tools on lines ~74-82 (e2e-test_echo and e2e-test_failing_tool), then fetches the audit log. The current assertions use .toBeGreaterThanOrEqual() which is too permissive — they'd pass even if extra entries leaked in from other operations.

Fix approach:
1. After fetching audit entries, filter by toolName to isolate entries from THIS test's operations. The audit entry schema likely includes a `toolName` field — read the fetchAudit helper and the audit entries to understand the structure.
2. Replace `expect(entries.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2)` with an exact count assertion on filtered entries.
3. For the success/failure filter (lines ~111-112), filter by the specific tool names and then check counts.
4. For line ~138, same approach — filter to the tool being tested.

The key insight is that the MCP server maintains an in-memory audit log of the last 500 invocations (see platform/mcp-server/CLAUDE.md). In tests using fixtures, each test gets an isolated MCP server, so the audit log should only contain entries from the current test. If that's the case, exact counts are safe. Verify by checking whether the fixture creates a fresh server per test.

userStories.title: Tighten analyze-site endpoint detection assertions
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want analyze-site.e2e.ts assertions to verify specific expected endpoints rather than just count >= 1, so that regressions in API detection are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Endpoint count assertions in e2e/analyze-site.e2e.ts use toContainEqual() or exact count checks based on the known test server scenarios, instead of toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)
Auth method detection assertions similarly use specific expected values from the test server configuration
Tests still pass against the current analyze-site test server behavior

userStories.title: Add try-finally cleanup for raw WebSocket in dispatch-resilience malformed-message test
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the raw WebSocket connection in dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts to be cleaned up in all code paths so that test failures do not leave orphaned connections.
acceptanceCriteria: The raw WebSocket created around line ~747 in e2e/dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts is wrapped in a try-finally block that closes it on both success and failure paths
The rawWs.close() call at line ~791 is moved into the finally block
The waitForExtensionConnected call after the close is also in the finally or after the try-finally

userStories.title: Fix resource cleanup ordering in sdk-version standalone test
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want sdk-version.e2e.ts standalone tests to guarantee cleanup even when startMcpServer succeeds but subsequent operations fail before the try block.
acceptanceCriteria: In e2e/sdk-version.e2e.ts, the standalone test (lines ~83-122) has server creation inside the try block or uses a pattern that guarantees cleanup on any failure path
If startMcpServer succeeds but a subsequent operation throws before the try block, the server is still killed
